title: Only generate the varlink glue code if needed and from the vendor dir

comments.body: thanks @haraldh , when #670 merges, I will test this out.
This has the notable advantage of removing two `go get` from builds, which should cut down on network related test flakes

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
#672 is why using vendor/ for anything but libraries is a bad idea. The tooling is not made to do this.

IMO, we should remove both `go get` from `varlink_generate` and move them to `install.tools`. This will solve the issue locally. CI will still hit it, but we can bump the vendored varlink code when that happens.

This is better than what we're doing now. We can optimize the build process further later.
LGTM.

:pushpin: Commit 443d0ca has been approved by `baude`
